method_atom: a wave-carrying medium threaded by a background flow whose speed varies in space; wherever the flow speed exceeds the local group velocity of the medium's excitations, an effective trapping horizon forms that confines propagating quanta on one side, and such a horizon spontaneously emits a thermal spectrum of those quanta — treated here for a discrete-lattice field theory with a nonlinear dispersion relation, together with a scheme to detect the emitted signal.
object_atom: phonon excitations of a ring of trapped ions set rotating with a stationary but inhomogeneous velocity profile, serving as a laboratory analogue in which supersonic regions mimic a black-hole horizon and its Hawking radiation.
brief_reason: The horizon-formation-from-supersonic-flow and thermal-emission-of-quanta machinery is field-neutral and transferable; the rotating trapped-ion ring with its phonons is the domain-specific object it is applied to.
